name: paper-citation.model
anchor_role: Classify papers by relationship to Theophysics work: represent, close, cite, contrast, or reject.
non_goals: Do not cite a paper only because it sounds adjacent.
Do not represent a paper as supporting Theophysics unless the claim match is explicit.
Do not bury opposition papers; classify them as contrast when useful.
verification: Each citation recommendation has quote/section/page locator where available.
Each paper receives one primary relation and optional secondary relations.
Near-match papers include the exact shared claim structure, not just keywords.
notes: This lane answers David's question: which papers are us, near us, useful to cite, useful to contrast, or noise?
